_fixture_metadata.source_ref: MIP-27 §7 (Transactions/serialization: unchanged on the wire). Under Supernova once execution is notarized, the tx endpoint response is structurally identical to Andromeda — status gets populated, receipt may be present, only the timing differs.
_fixture_metadata.purpose: **F2a tx-side input.** Read alongside block_settling_fail.json. The tx endpoint shows status='fail' at T₃ (after the settling block notarized the InvalidBlock miniblock). The dual-schema reader uses this tx response ONLY to correlate the tx_hash to the settling block — the AUTHORITATIVE failure signal is in lastExecutionResult on block N+1.
caveats: IMPORTANT FOR THE TEST: V1-F2a must verify that failure detection works EVEN IF the tx endpoint response lags the block endpoint. I.e. the dual-schema reader, given a settling block with an InvalidBlock miniblock and a tx endpoint response that still shows status='pending' (not yet caught up), MUST still emit AnchorFailedEntry based on the block-side signal. This fixture shows the happy case where both agree; the test harness MUST also construct a lagging-tx-endpoint variant by temporarily holding this fixture at status='pending' while presenting block_settling_fail.json. See V1-F2 prompt §4.

MIP-27 does not explicitly state whether a failed tx's receipt under Supernova is inline in the tx response (as in Andromeda) or lives in a separate endpoint. This fixture inlines the receipt; the dual-schema reader MUST tolerate both shapes (present and absent).
